title: Hash-pin PyYAML in the Weltgewebe Agent Safety workflow
goal: Make the Weltgewebe Agent Safety dependency installation reproducible and fail closed by binding PyYAML to reviewed package hashes instead of relying on a version constraint alone.

approval.note: Workflow and dependency changes require exact package provenance, current-main CI and no weakening of the Agent contract gate.

acceptance.assertion: The exact supported PyYAML distributions are recorded with reviewed SHA-256 hashes, Python and platform compatibility, and an update procedure that cannot silently widen the accepted artifact set.
The Agent Safety workflow installs PyYAML with hash verification and fails before contract validation when the artifact, version or hash differs from the reviewed set.
Tests or a reproducible local workflow fixture prove that a wrong hash, missing hash and unlisted distribution are rejected without fallback to an unhashed installation.
All Agent contract, schema, readiness and generated-artifact checks remain unchanged in authority and pass on current main after the dependency hardening.

rollback.strategy: Revert only the dependency installation change to the last reviewed version-pinned workflow while preserving all Agent Safety checks; never bypass the workflow on package resolution failure.

metadata.source_finding: The workflow pins PyYAML by version but not by package hash.
